description:
The species monitoring programme meetnetten.be aims at collecting reliable information on priority species in Flanders. It consists of a series of monitoring schemes which were designed by the Research Institute for Nature and Forest (INBO). Species are counted by volunteers in a standardized way under the coordination of the NGO Natuurpunt.

This report shows the results of the dragonfly monitoring schemes for the period 2016 - 2020. In Flanders we see following trends:

  • a strong increase in abundance for the Common Clubtail (Gomphus vulgatissimus) and the the Lilypad Whiteface (Leucorrhinia caudalis)
  • a decrease in abundance for the Beautiful Demoiselle (Calopteryx virgo) and the Yellow-spotted Whiteface (Leucorrhinia pectoralis)
  • a strong decrease in abundance for the Spearhead Bluet (Coenagrion hastulatum), the Crescent Bluet (Coenagrion lunulatum) and the Spotted Darter (Sympetrum depressiusculum)

The trend for the Variable Bluet (Coenagrion pulchellum) and Green-eyed Hawker (Aeshna isoceles) is uncertain. Starting from 2026 the monitoring sites for the Variable Bluet will be counted yearly, which will increase the precision of the trend estimates.
title: Trends op basis van de libellenmeetnetten (2016 - 2025). Citizen science monitoring in Vlaanderen.
